skill.name: deep-research
skill.description: Execute autonomous multi-step research using Google Gemini Deep Research Agent for market analysis, competitive landscaping, literature reviews, technical research, and due diligence.
skill.summary: Autonomous research agent powered by Google Gemini that plans, searches, and synthesizes comprehensive reports.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer detected pattern matches for external commands, network access, and environment variables. All findings are FALSE POSITIVES - the SKILL.md file is purely documentation showing command-line usage examples for a legitimate Python research tool. No executable code or malicious patterns exist. The skill uses Google Gemini API legitimately for research tasks.

content.value_statement: Manual research is time-consuming and often incomplete. This skill automates multi-step research using Google Gemini Deep Research Agent to deliver comprehensive, cited reports in minutes.
actual_capabilities: Execute autonomous multi-step research tasks using Google Gemini Deep Research Agent
Generate comprehensive research reports with citations and structured output
Support for streaming progress and status monitoring of long-running research
Continue research from previous sessions with context preservation
Output in multiple formats including markdown, JSON, and raw API responses
limitations: Requires a valid Google Gemini API key to function
Research tasks take 2-10 minutes depending on complexity
Costs $2-5 per task based on token usage
Requires Python 3.8+ and httpx package installed

use_cases.title: Market Analysis Reports
use_cases.description: Generate comprehensive market research including size, trends, key players, and growth opportunities for business planning and investment decisions.

use_cases.title: Technical Literature Reviews
use_cases.description: Automate the process of reviewing academic papers, technical documentation, and research to synthesize findings into coherent summaries.

use_cases.title: Competitive Intelligence
use_cases.description: Research competitors, compare products and technologies, and generate comparison tables for informed decision-making.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Research Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Research the history and evolution of Kubernetes from its origins to present day, including key milestones and major adopters.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick background research on a technology topic

prompt_templates.title: Comparative Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare Python web frameworks (Django, Flask, FastAPI) across performance, ease of use, ecosystem, and best use cases. Include a comparison table.

prompt_templates.title: Market Research with Streaming
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the electric vehicle battery market including key manufacturers, technology trends, supply chain dynamics, and 5-year projections. Stream progress updates.
prompt_templates.scenario: In-depth market analysis with real-time monitoring

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Session Research
prompt_templates.prompt: Continue the previous research on AI chip market. Elaborate on point 2 regarding NVIDIA's competitive positioning and emerging competitors.
prompt_templates.scenario: Follow-up research building on previous context

output_examples.output: A comprehensive markdown report covering Kubernetes origins at Google, open source release in 2014, CNCF adoption, major version releases, enterprise adoption timeline, and current ecosystem state with citations.

output_examples.input: Compare Python web frameworks with structured output
output_examples.output: Structured report with Executive Summary, Comparison Table (features, performance, learning curve), individual framework analysis, and Recommendations based on use case scenarios.

best_practices: Set the GEMINI_API_KEY environment variable before running research tasks
Use the --stream flag for long-running research to monitor progress in real-time
Specify structured output format with --format for programmatic consumption of results
anti_patterns: Running research without estimating time and cost - always inform users of 2-10 minute duration and $2-5 cost
Using --no-wait without implementing a follow-up status check mechanism
Expecting instant results - deep research is inherently a multi-step process that takes time

faq.question: How do I get a Gemini API key?
faq.answer: Visit Google AI Studio at aistudio.google.com and create an API key. Free tier available with usage limits.

faq.question: How long does a research task take?
faq.answer: Typically 2-10 minutes depending on query complexity. Simple queries complete faster while comprehensive market analysis takes longer.

faq.question: What is the cost per research task?
faq.answer: Costs range from $2-5 per task based on complexity. Token usage averages 250k-900k input and 60k-80k output tokens.

faq.question: Can I continue research from a previous session?
faq.answer: Yes, use the --continue flag with the previous interaction_id to build on earlier research with full context.

faq.question: What output formats are supported?
faq.answer: Default is human-readable markdown. Use --json for structured data or --raw for unprocessed API responses.

faq.question: How do I monitor long-running research tasks?
faq.answer: Use --stream for real-time progress updates or --status with the interaction_id to poll for current status.
